project.name: TradeLab
project.summary: TradeLab is a multi-asset crypto paper-trading platform for demo execution, strategy experimentation, and trading workflow validation.

package_management.frontend_resolution_note: legacy-peer-deps is pinned to keep local installs, GitHub Actions, and Docker builds aligned while the current Clerk dependency chain remains stricter than the repo's React patch level.

runtime_components: GitHub release and image publication pipeline
key_workflows: demo session creation
guest session persistence in sessionStorage only
guest-to-registered account upgrade with preserve-or-fresh choice
registered account bootstrap through Clerk-backed identity and HttpOnly app-session establishment
registered account logout with app-session revocation
authenticated paper-trading API access
server-authoritative market buy and sell execution
global accounting-mode selection across portfolio and history
focused trading flow through /markets/[symbol]
one strategy bundle per wallet and market
in-process strategy evaluation with database-backed claiming
strategy-driven dip-buy, take-profit, and stop-loss automation
human-readable bot reasoning in the activity log
market-data fetch with bounded stale fallback
structured backend request and service logging
PR-first CI validation
automatic master-image publication for development after merges to master
manual GitHub release execution from master
manual production promotion to the latest official release tag
k3s platform bootstrap with Argo CD app-of-apps
repo-managed MetalLB and Traefik platform synchronization
immutable release manifest rendering

constraints.delivery_model: Feature branches -> PR CI -> merge to master -> publish master images for dev -> bot PR updates Argo dev target -> CI -> merge -> manual backfill via workflow_dispatch when needed -> release
maintenance_expectations: Keep tests aligned with behavior changes.
Adjust structured logging when backend control flow changes.
Update documentation and docs/ai-metadata.json when repository structure, onboarding, workflows, or infrastructure bootstrap materially change.
Update GitHub Actions when quality gates, delivery behavior, or infrastructure validation need to evolve.

disclaimers.financial: TradeLab does not provide financial advice or investment recommendations.
disclaimers.ai_assistance: The repository includes AI-assisted code and documentation.

api_highlights.auth_model: Guest routes use backend-issued bearer tokens scoped to the browser session, while registered routes use Clerk-backed bootstrap plus a TradeLab HttpOnly app-session cookie
api_highlights.order_contract: POST /api/v1/orders accepts explicit side-specific payloads for buy and sell execution, including strategy-sourced orders internally
api_highlights.portfolio_query_contract: Portfolio and order-history reads accept accounting_mode, with orders and activity also supporting market_symbol filtering for focused screens
api_highlights.strategy_contract: Strategy routes manage one configurable automation bundle per wallet and market, with status, config, and last evaluation state

github_actions_sequence.action: Squash merge pull request into master

jobs_order: Build and publish backend/frontend master images
Create a bot-authored pull request that updates tradelab-dev to the source commit SHA plus matching immutable master image tags
github_actions_sequence.manual_backfill: Dispatch the workflow with source_ref=master when the current merged master state needs development images and a matching development-target update pull request backfilled

conditions: operator starts the workflow from master or another explicitly selected ref
jobs_order: Build backend binaries, Build frontend artifact, Publish backend image, and Publish frontend image

conditions: requested release tag exists as an official GitHub release or the latest release is resolved automatically
github_actions_sequence.action: Update the production Argo CD application to the selected official release tag on master

observability: Request lifecycle logging in the HTTP layer
Service-level logging for sessions, market data, orders, portfolio, and history

path_aliasing.mechanism: Traefik strip-prefix middleware entrypoints landing on the canonical root-served application

environment_revision_policy.development: track the latest merged master commit through an Argo application manifest committed on master
environment_revision_policy.production: track a promoted official release tag

user_workflows: Open the app and create a guest demo session automatically.
Inspect the default XRP/USDT reference market and chart feed state.
Switch the global accounting mode between average cost, FIFO, and hybrid.
Open the dedicated /markets/[symbol] screen for focused trading.
Configure and activate a strategy bundle from the market detail screen.
Inspect the last automation decision, outcome, and reason.
Review balances, positions, realized PnL, unrealized PnL, orders, and activity after execution.

local_first_run_order: open the app and confirm guest demo session creation
first_run_acceptance: a guest demo session is created automatically
the durable account prompt appears after the dashboard has loaded when auth is available
a demo buy updates balances, positions, orders, and activity
a market detail route opens for the selected pair
a demo sell updates positions and PnL

identity_roadmap.current_state: guest demo sessions plus Clerk-backed registered accounts with TradeLab-managed app sessions
identity_roadmap.target_state: guest mode plus hardened Clerk-backed registered accounts with app-session expiry, revocation, and redaction controls

contribution_expectations: Behavior changes should include tests when practical.
Documentation should stay aligned with product and process changes.
docs/ai-metadata.json should be updated when public repository structure changes.
Security issues should not be reported through public issues.
public_repo_standards.merge_model: pull request review plus squash merge to master

ticket_first_policy.rule: When work is not already covered by a concrete issue, create one before implementation.
preferred_sources: new GitHub issue created before code or documentation changes

pr_branch_currency_policy.rule: If master advances while a pull request is open, refresh the PR branch to the current master state before final merge or auto-merge.

change_set_completion_policy.rule: Completed change sets should be pushed promptly and attached to an updated or newly created pull request.

followup_artifact_policy.rule: When a change affects behavior, UI, delivery, or repository process, review and update the corresponding tests, logging, documentation, workflows, and docs/ai-metadata.json.

reproducibility_policy.rule: Prefer repository-tracked, reproducible fixes through code, manifests, workflows, and documentation over one-off manual cluster or environment changes.

ticket_execution_policy.rule: Every implementation should map to a concrete GitHub issue or an explicitly documented PR slice before code changes begin.

ticket_planning_policy.rule: Before implementation, record the intended scope, root cause, acceptance criteria, and PR grouping in the issue body or a planning comment.

ticket_progress_policy.rule: Track ticket progress through body plus comments: the body holds stable problem and acceptance context, while comments record phase transitions for the active PR slice.

phase_1.focus: Installation, first configuration, and first run

phase_3.focus: Security posture for sessions, credentials, and secrets

locked_decisions.strategy_bundle_scope: one bundle per wallet and market
locked_decisions.sell_scope: take-profit and stop-loss close the full open position
locked_decisions.explanations: user-facing activity should explain automated actions in plain English

conditional_followups: refresh screenshots when visible dashboard or workflow visuals change materially
validation_expectations: verify screenshots still match the visible UI
verify user-guide workflow text still matches the screen

conditional_followups: update docs/onboarding-requirements.md when the first-run or guest-flow behavior changes
validation_expectations: verify the documented user path matches the actual UI flow

required_followups: update README.md sample API flow if public usage changes
conditional_followups: update frontend_tests and e2e_tests if the frontend consumes the new or changed route
update docs/getting-started.md or docs/installation-validation.md if the first-run smoke path changes
validation_expectations: verify API examples and protected-route expectations remain correct

conditional_followups: update README.md and docs/getting-started.md if first-run identity behavior changes
update docs/system-operations.md if trust boundaries or runtime responsibilities change
update frontend_tests, backend_tests, and e2e_tests when implementation changes land
validation_expectations: verify guest versus registered behavior is still documented consistently

conditional_followups: update user and operator docs if visible or operational behavior changes
validation_expectations: verify behavior and logging remain aligned

conditional_followups: update API, user, or operator docs if the model change becomes externally visible
validation_expectations: verify docs/data-model.md matches the persisted model

conditional_followups: update docs/system-operations.md when runtime responsibilities or operator checks change
validation_expectations: verify required versus optional parameters are still documented correctly

conditional_followups: update docs/installation-validation.md when smoke validation changes
update README.md when the public delivery story changes
update deploy/infrastructure application image overrides when GitOps image selection changes
validation_expectations: verify deployment instructions still match manifests and release behavior

conditional_followups: update README.md when the public platform story changes
update deploy/kubernetes overlays when ingress class, hostnames, or cluster assumptions change
validation_expectations: verify MetalLB pool, Traefik ingress class, hostless path-based entrypoints, Argo applications, and generated secret behavior stay aligned

conditional_followups: update deploy/infrastructure applications when Argo CD ownership changes
update production-external-secrets overlay when the secret contract changes
validation_expectations: verify no committed default password remains in active overlays
verify generated secrets or external-secret paths still satisfy the workload contract

conditional_followups: update docs/getting-started.md or docs/developer-guide.md when contributor steps change
validation_expectations: verify PR -> CI -> merge -> release documentation remains accurate

conditional_followups: update README.md or docs/developer-guide.md if public contribution expectations change
validation_expectations: verify public repository standards still match GitHub configuration

required_followups: update README.md doc index and links
conditional_followups: update other docs that link to moved or new sources of truth
update README.md when public quality gates or trust signals change
validation_expectations: verify documented quality gates match CI

examples.scenario: Add or change trading actions such as buy, sell, or market detail flows
followups: update frontend tests and e2e tests

examples.scenario: Add or change strategy automation behavior
followups: refresh screenshots if automation UI changed
update backend tests, frontend tests, and e2e tests
update API examples or public docs if relevant

examples.scenario: Change first-run setup or required environment variables
Change how master merges are built and delivered to the development cluster
The development cluster did not receive images for the current merged master state
followups: manually dispatch Publish Master Images with source_ref=master
verify GHCR now contains the expected master-<shortsha> images
verify the generated development-target pull request updated deploy/infrastructure/applications/tradelab-dev.yaml to the matching source commit and immutable image tags
verify the generated development-target pull request merged successfully
verify Argo CD syncs the updated development application

examples.scenario: Change the cluster bootstrap, ingress controller, or MetalLB pool
Change how database credentials are provided to Kubernetes
followups: update CI manifest validation if overlay paths changed

examples.scenario: Add a new GitHub template or process
Change the authentication model or social login providers
followups: review onboarding and operator docs for trust-boundary changes
